findings.issue: Tasks 2 and 3 both modify `src/proxy_addon.py` in Wave 1. Since Wave 1 tasks execute in parallel, this creates a merge conflict: Task 2 extends response() for universal JS injection, while Task 3 adds intercept logic to request(). Two agents writing to the same file simultaneously will produce conflicting changes.
findings.fix: Move Task 3 (Proxy-level geolocation API intercept) to Wave 2, or merge Tasks 2 and 3 into a single task that handles both proxy_addon.py modifications together. The simplest fix is to merge them — both are small, self-contained changes to the same file.

findings.issue: Task 3 (Proxy-level geolocation API intercept) has no Verify-smoke field. This task involves external API interception — a concrete curl smoke check is possible without deployment: start the proxy locally and send a test POST to googleapis.com/geolocation through it.
findings.fix: Add: `Verify-smoke: python -c "import mitmproxy; ..."` or a pytest integration test invocation that confirms the intercept returns fake coordinates. Even a single targeted pytest run (e.g., `pytest test/test_proxy_addon.py -k geolocation_intercept`) would suffice.

findings.issue: Task 2 (Universal geolocation JS injection) has no Verify-smoke field. This task modifies response() to inject JS on non-target domains — an integration test confirming the injection is present in a non-target domain HTML response is straightforward and already planned in Testing Strategy.
findings.fix: Add: `Verify-smoke: pytest test/test_proxy_addon.py -k geolocation_injection_non_target` (or equivalent targeted test) to confirm the geolocation-only payload appears on non-target domains but the full payload does not.

findings.issue: Project Knowledge files `.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/architecture.md` and `.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/patterns.md` do not exist. Standards compliance check (file paths vs. directory structure, naming conventions) was skipped.
findings.fix: No action required for this tech-spec. Consider initializing Project Knowledge via `init-project-knowledge` skill to enable automated compliance checks in future specs.

summary: One critical finding: Tasks 2 and 3 create a wave conflict by both modifying src/proxy_addon.py in Wave 1. This must be resolved before execution — merge the two tasks or move Task 3 to Wave 2. Two minor findings: Tasks 2 and 3 lack Verify-smoke fields for their external/integration-testable behavior. One suggestion about missing Project Knowledge files. Frontmatter, structure, decisions, risks, acceptance criteria, and task quality are all compliant with the template.
